factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal
factors.summary: US-Iran interim peace deal advancing toward formal signing eases geopolitical risk premium embedded in gold. Oil has shed >7% across two sessions, signaling reduced inflation/haven bid for precious metals.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS yield data in news window; however, falling oil prices and reduced inflation expectations from Iran deal typically support lower real yields, which is bullish for gold. Awaiting FOMC clarity on rate trajectory.

factors.name: Fed Policy (FOMC Decision Today)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC decision imminent; market expects hold, but new Chair's initial communication will be critical for gold. Options traders split on near-term rate path; no hawkish surprise apparent in positioning, but high uncertainty.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: USD weakening on falling oil prices and lack of rate hike expectations. Weaker dollar is structurally bullish for gold, but magnitude unclear; DXY near 99.50.

factors.name: ECB/BoJ Policy
factors.summary: ECB signaled one more hike in September and higher-for-longer rates through 2027, supporting euro and real yields globally. BoJ hiked; yen weak despite, with USD/JPY near 160.47 (intervention threshold), signaling carry-trade dynamics still bearish for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Oil collapsed >7% on Iran deal news, now at three-month lows. Lower energy prices reduce inflation expectations and near-term safe-haven demand for gold, weakening bullish inflation narrative.

factors.name: Equity Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: NASDAQ fell -1.15% on Tuesday; S&P down -0.57%. Tech weakness and risk-off sentiment typically compete with gold safe-haven flows. SpaceX/AI euphoria dominating, not classic risk-off environment.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-Escalation
factors.summary: US-Iran peace deal framework removes a major source of geopolitical risk premium that supported gold. Physical demand from risk hedging likely to ease in near term.

factors.name: Japan Economic Data
factors.summary: Japan exports +17.0% y/y in May (beat +16.2% expected), business sentiment rising on semiconductor demand. Stronger Japan data neutral to slightly positive for yen stability and regional growth, no major gold implication.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: Gold up only +0.04% in 4h and +0.26% in 24h—minimal momentum despite Iran de-escalation news. Move has not confirmed bullish follow-through; consolidating at resistance near $4,335.

screening.narrative: Gold is consolidating near $4,355 after a modest bounce from multi-week lows, caught between competing narratives: the US-Iran peace deal and falling oil prices are removing geopolitical risk and inflation fears (bearish for gold), while a weakening US Dollar and Fed rate-hold expectations (via Warsh's first decision today) should support it. The lack of aggressive price action (+0.26% in 24h) suggests the market is in a holding pattern ahead of the FOMC announcement and waiting for real-yields guidance. Until the Fed clarifies its rate path and inflation trajectory, gold remains range-bound, with downside risks from prolonged geopolitical de-escalation and upside capped by softer risk sentiment in equities.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Policy Decision & Warsh Statement (today, ~18:00 UTC)
Fed Chair Warsh's Q&A commentary on rates, inflation, QT pace
US inflation expectations revision post-Iran deal
Oil prices continued weakness/EIA inventory data
USD/JPY intervention risk if pair stays >161 (affects carry unwind, could spike volatility)
Next CPI/PCE print timing and expectations for June
screening.risk_warning: If FOMC signals hawkish surprise (rate hikes rather than holds, or faster QT), or if Iran deal collapses triggering geopolitical re-escalation, this bias flips sharply bullish for gold. Conversely, if Fed dovish guidance becomes entrenched AND equity risk sentiment suddenly turns deeply negative, gold could sell off as real yields fall while equities crater (risk-off without safe-haven bid). BoJ intervention or USD/JPY reversal above 162 could spike volatility unpredictably.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway but shallow; recommend WAIT for FOMC clarity before committing. On retest of $4,330–$4,340 support post-decision, reassess bias based on real-yield reaction. Do not chase current consolidation; late entry risk is elevated given imminent catalyst.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window. Market awaits Fed decision today (Warsh) which will anchor real yield expectations; any surprise dovish tilt would be bullish gold.

factors.name: Fed policy decision (TODAY)
factors.summary: FOMC decision imminent (later 17 Jun). No change to rates expected per investinglive summary; market will focus on dot plot and forward guidance under new Chair Kevin Warsh. High uncertainty until release.

factors.name: USD strength (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY weakened to near 99.50 on US-Iran peace deal optimism. Weaker dollar is bullish gold, but move appears partially priced in already (mentioned as repositioning ahead of FOMC).

factors.name: Inflation data (UK CPI)
factors.summary: UK May CPI held at +2.8% y/y (vs +3.0% expected), missing upside. Core CPI +2.6% y/y vs +2.7% expected. Cool inflation is mildly bullish gold but UK-specific, not US inflation trend.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (US-Iran deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran framework agreement including $300bn fund and ceasefire relief; Strait of Hormuz reopening reducing oil-driven inflation fears. Lower energy risk = lower Fed hike risk = supportive for gold longer-term, but near-term uncertainty on implementation.

factors.name: Oil prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI near $75.80 (down ~5% previous day) on Hormuz reopening hopes and Iran supply returning. Lower oil reduces inflation pressure, which is ultimately bearish gold (less Fed urgency to hike = lower real yields, but also lower risk premium).

factors.name: Central bank policy (BoJ, ECB)
factors.summary: BoJ raised rates to 3-decade highs; ECB officials signaling more hikes ahead despite peace deal. BoJ tightening supports JPY (pressures USD/JPY below 160.50), mildly supportive for gold. ECB hawkishness is neutral to slightly bearish.

factors.name: Risk sentiment
factors.summary: Mixed signals: peace deal optimism supporting risk-on (EUR/USD +0.1610, AUD/USD above 0.7050), but traders cautious ahead of FOMC. Gold trading 'with mild negative bias' per FXStreet, suggesting some risk-on unwind of safe-haven premium.

factors.name: Gold ETF/physical flows
factors.summary: No specific GLD or physical demand data in window. Rabobank notes rising central bank gold demand and de-dollarization as structural bid, but no new positioning flow data.

factors.name: Price action (XAU/USD)
factors.summary: Gold edges lower during Asian session, holding above $4,300 but with 'mild negative bias' per FXStreet. Bulls lightening bets ahead of FOMC, suggesting uncertainty and lack of conviction into event.

screening.narrative: Gold is in a holding pattern ahead of the Fed decision today (Warsh FOMC). The US-Iran peace deal framework is reducing geopolitical premium and oil prices, which cuts into inflation expectations and limits gold's safe-haven appeal in the near term. Simultaneously, weaker USD on peace optimism provides modest support. However, traders are lightening long positions ahead of the event, and price action shows 'mild negative bias.' The outcome depends critically on whether the Fed signals continued or accelerated rate cuts — dovish surprise would flip bias bullish; hawkish surprise would extend weakness.
catalysts_ahead: Fed FOMC decision + dot plot + Warsh forward guidance (TODAY, 17 Jun ~18:00 UTC)
US May Retail Sales (concurrent with FOMC, 17 Jun)
ECB next meeting (TBD, but officials already hawkish)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if FOMC signals dovish pivot (rate cuts sooner/more aggressive) — would trigger immediate bullish reversal. Conversely, if Warsh surprises hawkish or holds hawkish forward guidance, gold could break below $4,285. Hormuz reopening execution delay or renegotiation could flip geopolitical risk back on, supporting gold. Move in gold already underway (price softening), so entry into further weakness requires conviction on Fed outcome.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in progress (mild downside). Avoid chasing; wait for FOMC decision at ~18:00 UTC, then enter on retest of $4,300 support (if result is hawkish) or on pullback toward $4,310–$4,315 if dovish surprise occurs. Do NOT enter now given imminent catalyst and repositioning chop.
